title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B parameter model achieves fourth-place quality on LM Arena while being significantly more efficient than comparable competitors, running on a single GPU instead of four or five. The model family is now available under Apache 2.0 license, enabling deployment on phones, hospitals, and edge devices like Mac computers.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4, focusing on ownership and deployment with open models. The 31B parameter model reaches fourth place on LM Arena while running on a single GPU rather than the four or five required by comparable competitors. The family ships under an Apache 2.0 license, which the talk frames as the basis for deployment on phones, in hospitals, and on edge devices including Mac computers. The throughline is that efficiency and a permissive license together enable on-device and self-hosted use without dependence on external providers.
